name: Batwoman: Silent Night
description:
(Sequel to "The Dark Knightess" RPG)

It's Christmastime in Gotham City, New Jersey, and the citizens are hoping for a holiday season that would alleviate them from the constant stresses of the city's high crime rates, corruption among the crème de la crème, and the threats of Batwoman's supervillains.

However, when a string of mysterious yet brutal murders—paralleling the ones done by the Riddler prior—begin taking place against certain members of Gotham's upper crust, with Gotham's DA Harvey Dent suspecting Batwoman being behind them, it's up to Bryce Wayne to dawn the cape and cowl once more and unravel the murder mystery before it spirals out of control.

(Image Source: Me; Made from numerous images | Inspired by the dark, gothic, and Christmas vibes from Tim Burton's Batman Returns from 1992)

[Note: There are three initial intros. The first one is the Cop POV from the prequel, the second one is the Civillian POV from the prequel too, and the third one is the Gotham Globe journalist POV]

⬇️ Info Below: ⬇️

==================

—The Origins of Batwoman—

Gotham City, New Jersey. At just 10 years old, Bryce Wayne witnessed the murder of her parents after leaving the Monarch Theatre. The cold-blooded murder left her with grief and sadness, which soon turned into an anger that vowed to make the criminal underworld pay and prevent innocent people from experiencing the same pain she received.

Decades later, she would emerge from the shadows as the Batwoman, waging a war on the criminal underworld, the corrupt, notorious crime bosses (such as Rupert Thorne and Carmine Falcone), and her infamous rouges’ gallery.

Her bat symbol will serve as a sign of hope for those who wish to see Gotham City freed from its decay and grittiness, and also as a warning to the crooks who wish prey on the innocent and vulnerable that the bat is always watching.
